content.full_text:
City of Morro Bay

595 Harbor St. • Morro Bay, CA 93442 • 805-772-1214

April 17, 1992

To: John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca., 95804

Subject: Conflict of Interest Determination

Dear John,
    This letter is to follow up on my phone conversation with you last week.
    Until November 1991, I was employed by an Environmental Center that operated a non-profit recycling center. I earned more than $200 per year.
    In February 1992, the City Council formally decided by resolution to separate the City's garbage services contract from the City's curbside recycling services contract. The garbage contract has been completed and accepted by a local garbage service. The recycling contract is out for bid. It will be several months before a new curbside services contract is accepted. At present our current garbage hauler also operates the curbside recycling services until the new contract is completed.
    I am writing you to get an opinion on voting on garbage issues. I feel, since the City has made the decision to separate the contracts for garbage and recycling, that I may now vote on garbage issues without a conflict of interest.
    There will soon be a Council meeting agenda item dealing with requesting "one bag" service from the garbage company. I would like to be able to participate in this discussion since it does not involve recycling.
    Please let me know if you need any further information to make your recommendation. I await your response.


Sincerely,

Susan Mullen,
Councilmember


CITY HALL                                    FIRE DEPARTMENT                              PUBLIC WORKS
595 Harbor Street                            715 Harbor Street                            695 Harbor Street

HARBOR DEPARTMENT                            POLICE DEPARTMENT                            RECREATION AND PARKS
1275 Embarcadero                            850 Morro Blvd.                              1001 Kennedy Way

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank document with very faint text that is not legible enough to transcribe accurately. The page shows mostly white space with some barely visible markings or text in the upper right and lower portions, but the content is too faint to read with confidence.

[illegible]
